english: vert all mortals. — mṅag-gźúg (mngag gzhug) a servant, slave, but esp. a messenger of the gods.
to curse, to execrate; mṅan bsgráṅ-ba (mngan bsgrang ba) Cs. 'enumeration of curses'; but mṅan mi bgraṅ? (mngan mi bgrang?) Lex. w.e.

resp. for dbaṅ (dbang), might, dominion, sway; mṅa mdzád-pa (mnga' mdzad pa) to govern, to rule, la (la) over; mṅa brnyés-pa (mnga' brnyes pa) to have obtained power Glr.; mṅa sgyúr-ba (mnga' sgyur ba) Tar. id.; to possess (books, knowledge etc); to have mastered, to understand thoroughly; mṅa gsól-ba (mnga' gsol ba) 1. to name, nominate, appoint, rgyál-por (rgyal por) a king Pth.; btsún-mor (btsun mor) to declare a woman one's wife Glr. 2. to praise C.; bkra-śís mṅa gsól-ba (bkra shis mnga' gsol ba) C. to congratulate. — mṅa-táṅ (mnga' thang) power, might. — mṅa-bdág (mnga' bdag) ruler, master, owner, frq. — mṅá-ba (mnga' ba) 1. vb. resp. for yod-pa (yod pa), to be (to have), rgyál-po-la sras gsum mṅá-ste (rgyal po la sras gsum mnga' ste) the king having three sons Dzl.; btsún-poi skū-la bsnyuṅ mi mṅa lágs-sam (btsun poi sku la bsnyung mi mnga' lags sam) (I trust) your majesty is not unwell? Glr. 2. adj. (partic.) being owned by, belonging to, Dzl. 72, 3; having, owning, = daṅ ldán-pa (dang ldan pa), frq. — mṅa-mdzád (mnga' mdzad) = mṅa-bdág (mnga' bdag). — mṅa-źábs (mnga' zhabs) Glr., mṅa-óg (mnga' 'og) Glr., mṅa-rís (mnga' ris) Lexr. subject to; a subject.

p.n., in a wider sense the whole country round the sources and the upper course of the Indus and Sutledge, together with some more western parts; the Cashmere, English, and most western Chinese provinces, where Tibetans live; in a more limited sense mṅa-ris skor gsum (mnga' ris skor gsum) denotes Rutok, Guge, and Purang. — mṅa-ris-kám-bu (mnga' ris kham bu) C. (*pú-tiṅ* (*pu ting*) W.), dried apricots from Balti; mṅa-ris ču (mnga' ris chu), mṅa-ris gtsáṅ-po (mnga' ris gtsang po), also gyas-ru-gtsáṅ-po (gyas ru gtsang po), and rta-mčóg-ḱa-bab (rta mchog kha bab), the principal river of Tibet.
W. *ṅár-mo* (*ngar mo*), C. *ṅár-po* (*ngar po*), sweet, frq.; *mṅar gsum* (*mngar gsum*) the three sweets, sugar, molasses, and honey; cf. dkar gsum (dkar gsum).

resp. lhums (lhums) womb; mṅál-gyi drí-mas ma gós-par (mngal gyi dri mas ma gos par) not contaminated by the impurity of the womb (so all the Buddhas are not born like other mortals, but come forth out of the side of the breast); mṅal daṅ ldán-par gyúr-ba (mngal dang ldan par gyur ba) to be with child; mṅal mi bdé-bar gyúr-ba (mngal mi bde bar gyur ba) to be taken by the labours of childbirth; maí mṅál-nas byúṅ-nas rtág-par (mai mngal nas byung nas rtag par) constantly from one's birth; mṅál-du čágs-pa (mngal du chags pa) 1. the originating in the womb, conception, 2. the foetus or embryo Med.; mṅál-du tógs-pa (mngal du togs pa) a disease; mṅál(-du) gnás(-pa) (mngal du gnas pa) foetus, embryo Thgy.; mṅál-du ḥjúg-pa (mngal du 'jug pa) to enter the womb, relative to a Buddha: his incarnating himself, his assuming flesh; mṅál-du ḥdzin-pa (mngal du 'dzin pa) Wdn. to conceive, to be with child. — mṅál-ḱa (mngal kha) mouth of the womb, orifice of the uterus Med. — mṅal-grib (mngal grib) contamination of the womb; Cs. adds: original sin, yet prob. it signifies nothing more than mṅál-gyi drí-ma (mngal gyi dri ma) v. above; (the said contamination is considered to extend to the least contact with a woman in child-bed). — mṅál-sgo (mngal sgo) the canal of the uterus, vagina; also in a more special sense the extreme orifice of the vagina Med.; frq. without any immediate physiological reference, the same as mṅal (mngal), e.g. when the subject of re-birth is spoken of. — mṅal-túr (mngal thur) a spoon used in midwifery for extracting a dead fruit (in the artificial delivering of a live child the obstetric art in Tibet is rather helpless). — mṅal rlúgs-pa (mngal rlugs pa) abortion, mṅal rlúgs-par byéd-pa (mngal rlugs par byed pa) to cause abortion Cs.

conspicuous, visible, e.g. continents, because they stand out of the water; more frq. fig.: evident, manifest, clear, mṅón-par gyúr-ba (mngon par gyur ba) to become manifest; to be verified, proved, e.g. gold by refining Dzl. — Tibetan writers regularly translate the Ssk. abhi by mṅón-pa (mngon pa), hence čos mṅón-pa (chos mngon pa) Dzl., and mnón-pai bka (mnon pai bka) Pth., the Abhidharma (v. Köpp. I., 595; Was.), mṅon-pai sde-snod (mngon pai sde snod) Abhidharma-piṭaka, mṅon-pa-mdzod (mngon pa mdzod) Abhidharma koṣa (v. Burn. I. and Was.); as a vb.: to be evident, to appear clearly, bdén-par čis mṅon (bden par chis mngon), from what is it evident that it is true? Dzl.; gnód-par bgyid-du mṅón-no (gnod par bgyid du mngon no) they are evidently bent on doing mischief Dzl.; mṅón-du byéd-pa (mngon du byed pa) to manifest, to make
